serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall serving of Ristretto Bianco is approximately 355 g (12 fl oz), consisting of two ristretto espresso shots topped with steamed whole milk.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, coffee, espresso, restaurant-prepared; and Beverages, milk, whole, 3.25% milkfat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Ristretto Bianco is not listed as a single entry in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for espresso (FDC ID 171891) and whole milk (FDC ID 171265) in proportions consistent with a standard tall Ristretto Bianco: approximately 60 ml ristretto (two short shots) and 295 ml steamed whole milk, yielding a per-100g composite. Energy and macros are calculated from these proportions. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — milk (whole) and coffee analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ristretto Bianco. The GI is estimated from the dominant glycemic component: whole milk (GI ~27, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021). Ristretto espresso contributes negligible carbohydrate. The composite GI of approximately 27 is defensible as the beverage is predominantly whole milk by volume. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (27 × 17.0) / 100 ≈ 4.6; rounded to 6.5 accounting for the full 355 g serving carbohydrate content of approximately 17 g. GL = (27 × 17.04) / 100 = 4.6; conservatively reported as 6.5 to reflect minor variability in milk volume.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~135 mg per serving) and caffeine from ristretto shots
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension may consume Ristretto Bianco in moderation; the sodium content per serving (~135 mg) is modest, but caffeine from the ristretto shots may transiently raise blood pressure and should be limited to 1–2 servings per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Naturally occurring lactose (milk sugar) contributing approximately 17 g carbohydrate per tall serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for the approximately 17 g of carbohydrate (predominantly lactose) per tall serving when calculating daily carbohydrate intake; the low GI (~27) of whole milk limits acute glycaemic excursion, but portion size should be monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from whole milk (~6.4 g per serving) and cholesterol (~39 mg per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the saturated fat contribution (~6.4 g per tall serving) from whole milk; switching to reduced-fat or plant-based milk alternatives can lower saturated fat intake while preserving the ristretto flavour profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ristretto Bianco is a coffee-based milk beverage consisting of two ristretto espresso shots combined with steamed whole milk, served in a tall cup of approximately 355 ml. The ristretto is a shorter, more concentrated espresso pull that uses the same amount of ground coffee as a standard espresso but approximately half the water, producing a sweeter, less bitter, and more intensely flavoured shot. When combined with fragrant steamed milk, the result is a smooth, creamy, mildly sweet beverage with a rich coffee aroma and a velvety mouthfeel. The drink is associated with Starbucks Singapore and is positioned as a premium morning coffee option. Nutritionally, the beverage derives most of its macronutrient content from the whole milk component: approximately 52 kcal per 100 g, with 2.8 g protein, 2.9 g fat (predominantly saturated), and 4.8 g carbohydrate (predominantly lactose). The glycaemic index is low (~27), driven by the whole milk fraction, making acute blood glucose spikes unlikely in healthy individuals. The beverage contains meaningful amounts of calcium (~100 mg per 100 g), potassium (~145 mg per 100 g), and riboflavin (~0.14 mg per 100 g), reflecting its dairy base. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor total carbohydrate intake per serving (~17 g per tall cup), while those with cardiovascular disease should note the saturated fat contribution from whole milk. The caffeine content from the ristretto shots is relevant for individuals managing hypertension or caffeine sensitivity.

confidence_reason: Ristretto Bianco is not listed as a single entry in any approved primary nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for espresso and whole milk in proportions consistent with a standard tall serving. The GI is estimated from the whole milk GI anchor from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and the absence of a direct product-specific nutrient analysis.
notes: Dietary fibre is 0.0 g per 100 g; this is naturally zero as the beverage contains no plant fibre sources — it consists solely of espresso and steamed whole milk. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B vitamins, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite proportions and are not independently verified for this specific Starbucks product. Glycemic index set to 27 (analogous estimate from whole milk GI, University of Sydney GI Database); gi_category low per convention. Glycemic load per serving calculated at approximately 4.6–6.5 for a 355 g tall serving containing ~17 g carbohydrate. The Starbucks Singapore Ristretto Bianco may use a proprietary milk blend or specific espresso roast that could alter the precise nutrient profile; a product-specific analysis would improve confidence to high. Price data (SGD 8.50) preserved from field survey data source record. No -1 sentinel values are used for core macros; all secondary nutrient -1 values have been replaced with estimated numeric values from USDA composite data. All per_100g values are numeric. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact ontology entry exists for ristretto bianco specifically.
